response_text:
**Thesis (2–3 sentences)**
Essity is a defensive consumer-staples compounder trading at a modest 14.4x P/E with a 3.4% yield, and we already own it at a small unrealised gain. The new signal is exceptional: 512 MSEK of net insider buying in 90 days from seven distinct buyers — including the long-term anchor owner Industrivärden and Lunden — with zero sellers, layered fresh this week. When the most informed long-term holders are aggressively adding into a -9% three-month drawdown, that deserves attention.

**Positives**
- Massive, broad, zero-sell insider cluster (7 buyers, 512 MSEK gross, fresh 129 MSEK from Lunden last week) — among the strongest insider signals one can see on Large Cap.
- Industrivärden's participation is meaningful: they are a strategic anchor, not a trader, and they are sizing up at these prices.
- Quality fundamentals intact: 13.3% operating margin, 14.1% ROE, 7.9 BSEK FCF (4.4% FCF yield), manageable leverage (net debt/equity 34%).
- Price has stabilised (+5.6% 1m, back to 200-day MA) after the 3-month drawdown — insiders appear to be marking a floor.

**Concerns**
- Revenue trending the wrong way: -6.2% YoY and -3.9% 3y CAGR. This is not a growth story; the thesis depends on margin and capital-return discipline, not top-line.
- I cannot see the Q1 2026 report contents, so I don't know whether the revenue decline is FX, divestments, volume, or pricing — material distinction I'm flagging as unknown.
- Next earnings ~July 23 is a known event risk roughly 8 weeks out; any further weakness could re-test recent lows.
- Still ~10% below 52-week high — the market is clearly worried about something I can't fully diagnose from price data alone.

**Insider read**
This is a textbook strong-signal cluster: multiple independent informed buyers, no sellers, large absolute size, sustained over weeks and freshly extended. On a Large Cap defensive name, this magnitude of conviction from Industrivärden specifically is rare and worth weighting heavily.

**Price read**
Trading at 259.60 vs cost 257.40 (essentially flat for us), right at the 200-day MA, 10% off 52w high. Momentum has turned positive over 1 month. Not a bargain on multiples, but not stretched either — fair value with a strong qualitative tailwind.

**Verdict**
The insider cluster materially strengthens the case versus a week ago, but the revenue decline and the data I can't see (Q1 contents) argue for measured sizing-up rather than a maximum bet.

`HELD: thesis intact`
